exemplar.title: To what extent does fatigue effect the shooting accuracy of advanced female netball players aged 15-18 in Singapore

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does fatigue effect the shooting accuracy of advanced female netball players aged 15-18 in Singapore

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Clearly links investigation to personal experience as a netball shooter, showing genuine curiosity and ownership.
Independent design choices (variable selection, pilot trials, graph formats) demonstrate initiative throughout.
Rationale conveys strong personal significance and forward-looking application to coaching and athlete performance.
weaknesses: Limited reflective commentary on personal learning process beyond practical decisions.
Could further elaborate on how this investigation shapes her broader academic or career interests.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands meet the descriptors for band 2: the student drives the project with clear ownership, justifies personal significance with curiosity beyond the classroom, and shows consistent initiative in planning and presentation. No significant shortfalls were noted in personal engagement.

strengths: Research question is relevant, contextually specific and identifies target population and performance variable.
Comprehensive controlled-variable table and consideration of multiple confounders reflect thoughtful experimental design.
Background review cites peer-reviewed studies and connects literature to game conditions.
Procedures are clearly described and replicable.
weaknesses: Focus could be tightened by defining âto what extentâ with a quantifiable accuracy change.
Background omits key biomechanical literature on shooting technique.
Ethical section lacks explicit safety protocols (first-aid, hydration).
Methodology limited by subjective Borg scale and small shot sample size.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: On balance the investigation meets the descriptors for the middle of band 3â4: the research question is mostly focused, background is mainly relevant, methodology is largely appropriate with evident limitations, and there is some awareness of safety/ethical issues. The weaknesses prevent a band-5â6 award but justify a mark of 4.

strengths: Adequate paired pre-/post-fatigue data support the analysis.
Application of mean, standard deviation, t-test and coefficient of variation indicates sound processing intent.
Interpretative narrative correctly links negative mean change to fatigue effects.
Mathematical processes are clearly explained in context.
weaknesses: Errors in mean and t-test calculations undermine accuracy of results.
No formal propagation or quantification of measurement uncertainties.
Formatting inconsistencies (blanks, overlines) reduce clarity of raw data tables.
Discussion overlooks non-significance of statistical test, risking overclaiming.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work aligns with the descriptors for band 3â4: relevant data are processed using appropriate methods, but execution errors and limited consideration of uncertainty prevent a top-band result. A mark of 4 reflects balanced strengths and shortcomings.

strengths: Acknowledges key methodological limitations (subjective fatigue ratings, sample variability).
Compares findings to scientific context, detailing central versus peripheral fatigue mechanisms.
Offers realistic suggestions (control group, standardized fatigue induction, endurance training).
Conclusion aligns with research question and cites mean and SD as supporting evidence.
weaknesses: Conclusion overstates fatigue effect without addressing statistical insignificance.
Discussion of errors and limitations remains somewhat superficial.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All four strands sit in the middle band (3â4): conclusions are relevant but only partially supported, comparisons to context are reasonable, limitations are identified but not explored in depth, and suggestions are realistic yet under-specified. A mark of 4 accurately represents this performance.

strengths: Report follows a clear IB structure with labeled sections and justified formula presentation.
Tables and graphs aid understanding despite some resolution and formatting issues.
weaknesses: Frequent formatting errors in tables and figures hinder readability.
Digressions and excessive quotations obscure focus in places.
Spelling, grammar and citation inconsistencies reduce overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work fits the descriptors for band 1â2: presentation is mostly clear but marred by organization weaknesses, relevance is intermittently obscured by irrelevant content, and conventions are applied with several errors. A mark of 2 reflects these communication strengths and deficiencies.

strandSelections.reasoning: Error bars are plotted, yet no uncertainties for shot counts or Borg ratings are estimated or propagated.  Discussion of overlap is superficial, indicating **limited consideration of uncertainty**.
Central vs peripheral fatigue is explained and literature is referenced, providing a **reasonable but not deeply detailed** link to scientific context.
The work follows a logical IB structure (Background, Method, Analysis, Evaluation), but internal repetition and misplaced appendices weaken overall organisation, so structure is **weak but present**.

Proposals include adding a control group, standardising fatigue induction and improving endurance training.  These are realistic but lack detail on implementation or measurement, giving **moderately relevant suggestions**.

The question is relevant, specifies population, variable and performance measure, yet the phrase âto what extentâ is not matched by a clearly quantifiable definition of accuracy change, so focus is **mostly but not fully tight**.

Subjective Borg ratings, unequal running distances and non-shooters in the sample are acknowledged, showing **some methodological insight**, yet potential measurement error and statistical flaws are not explored fully.

Variables are identified and a controlled-variable table is provided, yet the fatigue protocol is subjective (Borg 9) and the shot sample is small, creating notable limitations; thus the methodology is **largely appropriate but with clear weaknesses**.

Two peer-reviewed studies are summarised and links to game conditions are drawn; however, physiological mechanisms are only sketched and key biomechanical literature on shooting technique is missing, so coverage is **mainly appropriate but not exhaustive**.
Frequent digressions (e.g., cardiovascular training advice) and lengthy quotations dilute focus, occasionally obscuring meaning; relevance is therefore **inconsistent**.
The narrative links a mean decrease to a negative effect and references fatigue mechanisms, but ignores the non-significant t-test and outliers, so interpretation is **broadly valid but incomplete**.

Sections are labelled and graphs included, yet many tables contain formatting errors (fractions, overlines, blanks) and some figures are low resolution, making parts of the report **difficult but still possible to follow**.

The student links the topic directly to her own experience as a netball shooter and designs a study centred on her sport.  She personally selects the performance variable, creates tables and graphs, and carries out preliminary trials, all of which indicate **clear ownership and independent choices** rather than following a teacher-provided template.
Mean, SD, t-test and CV are attempted; however the t-value is mis-calculated and units/signs are inconsistent.  Processing therefore shows **appropriate intent but execution errors**.

The rationale explicitly states why fatigue matters to the studentâs competitive performance and cites published work to show curiosity beyond the classroom.  The explanation of how findings could aid coaches and athletes demonstrates **strong personal significance and forward-looking interest**.
Twenty paired pre-/post-fatigue scores are provided, giving an adequate data set, yet several entries are blank or contain formatting symbols, so the raw record is **relevant but partly incomplete**.

The investigation plan, data tables, consent forms and PAR-Q are organised by the student; she also modifies the running distance after a pilot, showing initiative in refining the method.  Presentation choices (error-bar graphs, statistical tests) reinforce **consistent personal input across all stages**.

Consent, PAR-Q, confidentiality and right to withdraw are addressed, but risk mitigation (e.g., first-aid provision, hydration) is only implied.  This reflects **some but not complete awareness** of safety and ethics.

Basic SEHS terms are used correctly (central fatigue, SD) but there are spelling/grammar errors and mis-formatted formulae.  Citation style is inconsistent, so conventions are **applied with several errors**.

The conclusion matches the question and cites mean and SD as evidence, yet the statistical insignificance is overlooked, limiting full support.  Hence relevance is sound but evidence is **partially developed**.

annotations.comment: Change âeffect the shooting accuracyâ to âaffect shooting accuracyâ to use correct terminology.
Hypothesis and null hypothesis are clearly stated but could be formalized with Hâ and Hâ notation for statistical clarity.
Controlled variable table is comprehensive and demonstrates thoughtful experimental design.
The âfeeding the chickenâ and âclosing the gateâ items are unclearâuse standard exercise terminology to maintain professionalism.
Recognition of motivation, diet, and sleep as confounders shows methodological awareness.
Table layout is cluttered; align text consistently and reduce cell overflow to improve readability.
Consider adding a note on how you will verify participantsâ adherence to diet and sleep instructions.
Procedure clearly outlines steps, demonstrating replicability of the experiment.
The research question clearly specifies the target population and context, enhancing relevance to the investigation.
Refine âto what extentâ by defining a specific, quantifiable change in accuracy (e.g. percentage decrease) to tighten focus.
Personal narrative in the background shows genuine curiosity and self-motivation, reflecting strong personal engagement.
Background omits discussion of key biomechanical factors in shooting technique; include a concise summary of relevant kinematics literature.
Several sentences are lengthy and run-on, hindering clarity; break into shorter paragraphs and check punctuation.
Running until Borg stage 9 yields variable circuit distances and undermines method consistencyâstandardize running distance or use heart rate.
Ethical section mentions PAR-Q but omits first-aid and hydration protocolsâadd explicit safety mitigation.
Preliminary trial of one subject is a useful pilot but cannot support conclusionsâplan miniâsample of at least 3 for trial.
Raw data table has formatting anomalies (blanks, overlines) that compromise clarityâensure uniform numeric entries.
Interpretation of the pilot result overstates reliability; acknowledge the singleâsubject limitation in your discussion.
Change table of shot changes uses notation (overlines) that obscures participant IDsâuse plain numbers for ease of analysis.
Methodology rationale clearly explains each mathematical process, linking purpose to investigation context.
Include estimates of measurement uncertainty propagation when calculating mean and SD for fuller quantitative rigour.
The formula presentation is clear and student justifies each test appropriately, showing analytical understanding.
Mean calculation omits four data points; double-check sum and divisor to ensure accuracy of $$-1.0525$$ value.
Student correctly interprets negative mean change to indicate fatigueâs effect, demonstrating valid data interpretation.
T-test calculation lacks stated degrees of freedom and significance threshold; include $$df$$ and $$\alpha$$ to contextualize $$t=0.0813$$.
Coefficient of variation result is negative; interpret absolute dispersion or use absolute mean to avoid sign confusion.
Graph interpretation overlooks non-significant t-test; acknowledge this in the discussion to avoid overclaiming significance.
In Graph 1.5 label, â1.5â appears inexact; ensure consistent numbering (Graph 1.1 or 1.2) and clear axis titles.
Strength section highlights large sample and repeated measures, reinforcing data reliability.
Recommendation to add a non-fatigued control group is valid; expand by outlining specific control protocol.
Physiological explanation of central fatigue is detailed and well-linked to results, showing strong evaluation.
Conclusion overstates negative effect without addressing statistical insignificance; temper claims to reflect $$p>0.05$$ result.
Practical recommendations for coaches show insightful real-world application, demonstrating personal engagement.
Conclusion is lengthy and includes new recommendations; consider focusing on summarizing key findings and relocating recommendations to evaluation.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Justification shows little personal significance or curiosity
Little evidence of personal input in design, implementation or presentation
Limited evidence of personal engagement with little independent thinking
Clear evidence of personal input and initiative throughout the investigation
Clear evidence of personal engagement with significant independent thinking
Justification demonstrates clear personal significance and curiosity

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Evidence of personal engagement and independent thinking

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Superficial or limited relevance background information
Research question stated but not focused
Methodology appropriate to very limited extent
Mainly appropriate and relevant background information
Relevant but not fully focused research question
Entirely appropriate and relevant background information
Highly appropriate methodology addressing all significant factors
Relevant and fully focused research question
Sufficient relevant raw data supporting detailed conclusion
Basic outline of practical issues only
Some relevant comparison to scientific context
Detailed, relevant and fully supported conclusion
Clear understanding of methodological issues demonstrated
Correct and justified comparison to scientific context
Many errors in terminology and conventions
Appropriate use of terminology with minimal errors
Well structured with all necessary information
